title: Church & Dwight is Down 20% From Its High. Time to Buy?
text: Having lost a material amount of value, is this consumer staples maker with a modest 1.3% yield worth buying?

title: Church & Dwight Is A Good Buy At The Current Levels
text: Church & Dwight was adversely impacted by lower consumer spending in an inflationary environment in the third quarter. However, 80% of the business portfolio is experiencing healthy demand.

title: Pandemic Winners, Inflation Losers - Finally Time To Buy Clorox And Church & Dwight?
text: Clorox and Church & Dwight are undeniable winners from the pandemic. However, CLX and CHD are currently struggling with input cost inflation and other headwinds. In this article, I will take an in-depth look at the fundamentals of both companies. I explain which company is better managed and is the better long-term investment.

title: Dividend Stocks To Buy Now? 3 To Check Out This Week
text: 3 top dividend stocks to watch in December 2022.

title: Church & Dwight Webcasts Fireside Chat at the Morgan Stanley Global Consumer & Retail Conference
text: EWING, N.J.--( BUSINESS WIRE )--Church & Dwight Co., Inc. (NYSE: CHD) will participate in a fireside chat at the Morgan Stanley Global Consumer & Retail Conference on Tuesday, December 6 at 8:45 a.m. ET. A link to the broadcast will be provided through the Investors section of Church & Dwight's website at http://investor.churchdwight.com/investors/news-events.

title: 5 Soaps & Cleaning Materials Stocks Showing Potential Amid Hardships
text: The soap and cleaning materials industry battles headwinds from elevated logistics, input and manufacturing costs. Innovation, digital growth and brand building are likely to drive players like PG, RBGLY, HENKY, CHD and CLX.
